metadata.title: Flow-Specific Docker Image Tiers — 3-Tier Base Hierarchy
metadata.predecessor-prs: PR #542 — sec(docker): H3/H4 multi-stage builds, cap_drop, Trivy, seccomp PR #559 — feat(security): Docker Security Hardening 2nd Pass (seccomp enforce, SOPS, MLflow auth, Falco)

tier.name: GPU
tier.description: CUDA 12.6.3 runtime + PyTorch + MONAI + full ML stack. minivess-base:latest is NEVER modified by this plan. Built from Dockerfile.base (H3+H4 hardened, PR #542).

flow.name: analyze
flow.note: runs inference on served models from MLflow

flow.name: data
flow.note: transitively imports PyTorch via splits.py → loader.py → MONAI transforms

flow.name: acquisition
flow.note: future GPU ambitions per Draelos et al. 2025

tier.name: CPU
tier.description: python:3.13-slim-bookworm + scipy/pandas/matplotlib/DuckDB/MLflow stack. No PyTorch, no MONAI, no CUDA. Optimized for embarrassingly parallel CPU workloads (bootstrap CI, Bayesian analysis, figure generation). Target image size: ~1.5-2.5 GB (vs ~8-12 GB for Tier A).

tier.name: Light
tier.description: python:3.13-slim-bookworm + orchestration/web deps: prefect, mlflow-client, FastAPI, uvicorn, httpx, pydantic, matplotlib, duckdb. Target image size: ~1.0-1.5 GB (vs ~8-12 GB for Tier A). NOTE: matplotlib+duckdb make this ~1.0-1.5 GB, not ~500 MB. Still a 6-8x reduction from the GPU base.

tier.name: Dead Code
tier.description: QA flow was merged into dashboard health adapter (#342, PR #567). Dockerfile.qa, the qa compose service, and ALL references to qa as a flow must be removed. Blast radius is large (see R3 reviewer findings).
tier.blast-radius-files: deployment/docker/Dockerfile.qa (DELETE) deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml (remove qa service) src/minivess/orchestration/trigger.py (_DEFAULT_FLOWS, _best_effort) src/minivess/orchestration/deployments.py (qa entries) deployment/prefect/deployments.yaml (qa deployment block) scripts/weekly_security_scan.sh (minivess-qa scan target) tests/v2/unit/test_docker_per_flow.py (4 tests reference qa) tests/v2/unit/test_qa_flow.py (DELETE or skip entire file) tests/v2/unit/test_qa_flow_uri.py (DELETE or skip entire file) tests/unit/orchestration/test_no_cross_flow_imports.py (qa_flow in list) tests/unit/orchestration/test_prefect_decoupling_verification.py (qa_flow) tests/unit/orchestration/test_env_var_naming.py (qa_flow.py path) tests/v2/integration/test_no_ephemeral_outputs.py (qa_flow entry) tests/v2/unit/test_env_single_source.py (qa_flow) tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_security_tooling.py (minivess-qa scan) tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_dockerfile_flows.py (_EXCLUDED cleanup) src/minivess/orchestration/constants.py (EXPERIMENT_QA, FLOW_NAME_QA constants) tests/v2/unit/test_deployments_yaml.py ("qa-flow" in flow name list) Makefile (line 65: qa in the flow image scan loop) deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml (line 115: stale "qa" in comment) tests/v2/unit/test_flow_trigger.py (lines 79, 157: "qa" in flow/best_effort lists) tests/v2/unit/test_deployments_config.py (line 91: "qa" in required set) tests/v2/integration/test_docker_compose_volumes.py (lines 252-272: TestQaService class) tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_names.py (lines 86, 104: FLOW_NAME_QA import) tests/unit/orchestration/test_constants.py (lines 19-118: EXPERIMENT_QA, FLOW_NAME_QA) src/minivess/orchestration/flows/pipeline_flow.py (line 46: imports FLOW_NAME_QA, line 111: uses it) src/minivess/orchestration/agent_interface.py (line 110: DeterministicQATriage class — dead code) src/minivess/orchestration/CLAUDE.md (line 54: qa_flow.py in table) deployment/CLAUDE.md (line 270: "dashboard/qa/biostatistics" reference) tests/v2/unit/test_docker_per_flow.py (lines 70, 94, 166-170: qa in flow lists + test_chain_has_qa_flow) CLAUDE.md (lines 76, 124, 174, 476: "Flow 6: QA", Dockerfile.qa, "6 flows") README.md (lines 36, 102: QA listed as standalone flow) deployment/prefect/work-pools.yaml (lines 4, 13: QA in work pool comments)

build-strategy.rationale:
The original plan proposed rewriting pyproject.toml at build time with extract_tier_deps.py. Reviewer R2 identified this as fragile: - `uv sync --frozen` with a rewritten pyproject.toml bypasses lock validation - `uv sync --locked` would FAIL because the rewritten toml doesn't match the lock - The rewritten file is a lie — it doesn't represent the project's actual deps
SOLUTION: Use PEP 735 [dependency-groups] + `uv export --only-group`.

step.description: Define tier groups in [dependency-groups] section of pyproject.toml
Generate pinned requirements files from the lock
step.command:
uv export --frozen --only-group cpu --no-emit-project \ --output-file deployment/docker/requirements-cpu.txt
uv export --frozen --only-group light --no-emit-project \ --output-file deployment/docker/requirements-light.txt
step.note: These files are committed to git. Regenerated via `make requirements-tiers`. A pre-commit hook or CI check should verify they're up-to-date.

step.description: Dockerfile builder stage uses uv pip install -r
step.dockerfile-pattern: FROM python:3.13-slim-bookworm AS builder COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /usr/local/bin/uv WORKDIR /app COPY deployment/docker/requirements-{tier}.txt requirements.txt RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/uv \ uv venv /app/.venv && \ uv pip install --python /app/.venv/bin/python -r requirements.txt COPY src/ src/ COPY configs/ configs/
step.note: No pyproject.toml copy needed in tier base images. No uv sync, no --frozen mismatch risk. Pinned versions from the lock file via uv export. IMPORTANT: Runner stage must set ENV PYTHONPATH=/app/src so that `import minivess` works. This mirrors Dockerfile.base.

phase.name: Foundation: Dependency Groups + QA Full Cleanup
phase.description: Add [dependency-groups] cpu and light to pyproject.toml. Generate requirements-{tier}.txt files. Perform COMPLETE QA flow removal across all files (not just Dockerfile + compose).

task.title: Test: pyproject.toml has cpu and light dependency groups
task.description:
Write tests in tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_dependency_groups.py: 1. Parse pyproject.toml with tomllib 2. Assert [dependency-groups] contains 'cpu' and 'light' keys 3. Assert 'cpu' group does NOT contain torch, monai, torchio, torchvision, torchmetrics 4. Assert 'light' group does NOT contain torch, monai, scipy, numpy, torchio 5. Assert 'cpu' group DOES contain: scipy, pandas, matplotlib, duckdb, mlflow 6. Assert 'light' group DOES contain: prefect, mlflow, fastapi, pydantic 7. Assert requirements-cpu.txt exists in deployment/docker/ 8. Assert requirements-light.txt exists in deployment/docker/ 9. Assert requirements files do NOT contain 'torch' (case-insensitive, but allow 'torchmetrics' to NOT appear — just check 'torch==' or 'torch>=' is absent)
All tests are static file parsing — staging tier, no markers needed.

task.title: Add cpu and light dependency groups + generate requirements files
task.description:
1. Add [dependency-groups] cpu and light to pyproject.toml. Version constraints MUST match those in [project.dependencies] for shared packages (mlflow, prefect, pydantic, etc.) — copy-paste.
cpu group: numpy, scipy, pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, duckdb, mlflow, pydantic, nibabel, scikit-posthocs, pingouin, baycomp, statsmodels, prefect, httpx, psycopg2-binary, boto3, openlineage-python, psutil
light group: prefect, httpx, pydantic, mlflow, boto3, psycopg2-binary, fastapi, uvicorn, matplotlib, seaborn, duckdb, psutil
2. Run `uv lock` to regenerate uv.lock with the new groups.
3. Generate requirements files: uv export --frozen --only-group cpu --no-emit-project \ --output-file deployment/docker/requirements-cpu.txt uv export --frozen --only-group light --no-emit-project \ --output-file deployment/docker/requirements-light.txt
4. Verify requirements files do NOT contain torch/monai.

task.title: Test: QA flow fully removed from codebase
task.description:
Write tests in tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_qa_removed.py: 1. deployment/docker/Dockerfile.qa does NOT exist 2. docker-compose.flows.yml does NOT contain a 'qa:' service key 3. PipelineTriggerChain._DEFAULT_FLOWS does not contain "qa" (parse trigger.py with ast module — Rule #16) 4. deployment/prefect/deployments.yaml does not contain "qa" deployment 5. scripts/weekly_security_scan.sh does not scan "minivess-qa" 6. src/minivess/orchestration/constants.py does not define EXPERIMENT_QA or FLOW_NAME_QA 7. tests/v2/unit/test_deployments_yaml.py does not list "qa-flow" 8. Makefile scan target does not list "qa" as a flow image 9. src/minivess/orchestration/flows/pipeline_flow.py does not import FLOW_NAME_QA 10. tests/v2/unit/test_flow_trigger.py does not contain "qa" in flow lists 11. tests/unit/orchestration/test_constants.py does not import EXPERIMENT_QA or FLOW_NAME_QA 12. tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_names.py does not import FLOW_NAME_QA
All tests are static file parsing — staging tier.

task.title: Complete QA flow removal across entire codebase
task.description:
DELETE: - deployment/docker/Dockerfile.qa
MODIFY (remove qa references): - deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml → remove qa service block - src/minivess/orchestration/trigger.py → remove "qa" from _DEFAULT_FLOWS and _best_effort set. The dashboard now handles QA (#342). - src/minivess/orchestration/deployments.py → remove qa entries - deployment/prefect/deployments.yaml → remove qa deployment block - scripts/weekly_security_scan.sh → remove minivess-qa from image list - src/minivess/orchestration/constants.py → remove EXPERIMENT_QA and FLOW_NAME_QA - Makefile → remove "qa" from the scan flow image loop (line 65)
TEST FILES (remove qa test expectations): - tests/v2/unit/test_docker_per_flow.py → remove "qa" from all flow lists, remove test_qa_dockerfile_exists, update test_chain_has_qa_flow - tests/v2/unit/test_qa_flow.py → DELETE (dead tests for dead flow) - tests/v2/unit/test_qa_flow_uri.py → DELETE (dead tests for dead flow) - tests/unit/orchestration/test_no_cross_flow_imports.py → remove qa_flow - tests/unit/orchestration/test_prefect_decoupling_verification.py → remove qa_flow - tests/unit/orchestration/test_env_var_naming.py → remove qa_flow.py path - tests/v2/integration/test_no_ephemeral_outputs.py → remove qa_flow entry - tests/v2/unit/test_env_single_source.py → remove qa_flow - tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_security_tooling.py → remove minivess-qa - tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_dockerfile_flows.py → remove any qa exclusion - tests/v2/unit/test_deployments_yaml.py → remove "qa-flow" from flow name list - tests/v2/unit/test_flow_trigger.py → remove "qa" from flow_names/best_effort lists, update flow count assertion (9 → 8) - tests/v2/unit/test_deployments_config.py → remove "qa" from required set - tests/v2/integration/test_docker_compose_volumes.py → DELETE TestQaService class - tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_names.py → remove FLOW_NAME_QA import + assertions - tests/unit/orchestration/test_constants.py → remove EXPERIMENT_QA/FLOW_NAME_QA imports + tests - src/minivess/orchestration/flows/pipeline_flow.py → remove FLOW_NAME_QA import, remove "qa" from _DEFAULT_PIPELINE_STEPS, update docstring (9→8 steps) - src/minivess/orchestration/agent_interface.py → remove DeterministicQATriage class (dead code) - src/minivess/orchestration/CLAUDE.md → remove qa_flow.py from file table - deployment/CLAUDE.md → remove "qa" from "dashboard/qa/biostatistics" reference
DOCUMENTATION (update QA-as-standalone-flow references): - CLAUDE.md → update "Flow 6: QA" to show QA merged into dashboard, remove Dockerfile.qa reference, update "6 flows" to "5 flows + QA merged" - README.md → remove QA as standalone flow, note merged into dashboard - deployment/prefect/work-pools.yaml → remove "QA" from work pool comments
ALSO FIX (stale references): - deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml → line 115 comment "dashboard, qa," → remove "qa,"
PRE-EXISTING BUGS (fix opportunistically): - deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base → line 80 LABEL says "Python 3.12" → fix to "Python 3.13" - tests/v2/unit/test_docker_per_flow.py → line 56 checks "python:3.12" → fix to "python:3.13"
KEEP (for now): - src/minivess/orchestration/flows/qa_flow.py → the Python module stays until all importers are confirmed zero. It's dead code but harmless. Create a follow-up issue to track its eventual deletion.
task.post-verify: Run make test-staging to catch any remaining qa references. If any test fails with "qa" in the error message, fix it immediately.

task.title: Test: tracking.py resolve_tracking_uri is importable without torch
task.description:
Write test in tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_import_tiers.py: 1. Parse src/minivess/observability/tracking.py with ast module 2. Assert NO top-level import of minivess.serving.model_logger (it must be inside a function, not at module level) 3. Assert resolve_tracking_uri is still importable (basic import check)
This ensures Tier C flows can import resolve_tracking_uri without pulling in torch → CUDA → 8 GB of GPU dependencies.
Static file parsing — staging tier, no markers needed.
task.expected-result: FAIL (import is still at module level)

task.title: Make log_single_model import lazy in tracking.py
task.description:
In src/minivess/observability/tracking.py: - REMOVE line 26: `from minivess.serving.model_logger import log_single_model` - ADD lazy import inside the log_model() method at line ~660: `from minivess.serving.model_logger import log_single_model`
This breaks the torch import chain for all code that only uses resolve_tracking_uri (dashboard_flow, pipeline_flow, qa_flow, etc.) without affecting the training flows that actually call log_model().
VERIFIED: log_single_model is only called in one method (line 660). No other code in tracking.py references it.
task.expected-result: PASS (both tier isolation and existing tracking tests)

task.title: Commit: dependency groups + QA full cleanup + import chain fix
task.description: Stage and commit all Phase 1 changes. Message: "feat(docker): add cpu/light dependency groups, remove QA flow, break torch import chain"

phase.name: New Base Images
phase.description:
Create Dockerfile.base-cpu and Dockerfile.base-light following the same H3+H4 hardening pattern as Dockerfile.base (PR #542). Both use python:3.13-slim-bookworm as Layer 1 instead of nvidia/cuda.
BUILD STRATEGY (post-review): Use pre-generated requirements-{tier}.txt files (from Phase 1) with `uv pip install -r`. No pyproject.toml rewriting. No extract_tier_deps.py.

task.title: Test: new base Dockerfiles exist with correct structure
task.description:
Write tests in tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_base_image_tiers.py. ALL checks are STATIC TEXT ANALYSIS of Dockerfile content, NOT runtime.
Dockerfile.base-cpu: 1. File exists in deployment/docker/ 2. Uses python:3.13-slim-bookworm as builder base (NOT nvidia/cuda) 3. Uses python:3.13-slim-bookworm as runner base 4. Has multi-stage build (contains "AS builder" and "AS runner") 5. Has LABEL tier="cpu" 6. Does NOT contain "nvidia" or "cuda" anywhere 7. Has non-root user creation (contains "useradd" and "minivess") 8. Has HEALTHCHECK 9. Contains "requirements-cpu.txt" (uses the pre-generated requirements) 10. Contains "uv pip install" (NOT "uv sync")
Dockerfile.base-light: 11-19. Same checks but with tier="light" and requirements-light.txt
Cross-tier: 20. All three base Dockerfiles (base, base-cpu, base-light) exist 21. Only Dockerfile.base contains "nvidia/cuda"

task.title: Create Dockerfile.base-cpu
task.description:
Multi-stage build mirroring Dockerfile.base (PR #542) hardening:
Stage 1 (builder): FROM python:3.13-slim-bookworm AS builder - apt-get: gcc, python3-dev (for C-extension wheels: psycopg2, scipy) - COPY uv from ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest - COPY deployment/docker/requirements-cpu.txt - uv venv + uv pip install -r requirements-cpu.txt (with cache mount) - COPY src/ configs/
Stage 2 (runner): FROM python:3.13-slim-bookworm AS runner - ARG GIT_COMMIT, BUILD_DATE, UID, GID (same as Dockerfile.base) - OCI labels: tier="cpu", maintainer, project, source, revision, etc. - Non-root user: minivess (configurable UID/GID) - COPY --from=builder .venv, src/, configs/ - chmod g+rX, umask 0002 - ENV PATH, PYTHONPATH, PYTHONUNBUFFERED, PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE - HEALTHCHECK
NO nvidia/cuda references anywhere. NO apt-get in runner stage. NO uv binary in runner stage.
ALSO: Add "Dockerfile.base-cpu" and "Dockerfile.base-light" to _EXCLUDED set in test_dockerfile_flows.py (they are base images, not flow images — the glob would pick them up and fail flow checks).

task.title: Create Dockerfile.base-light
task.description:
Same multi-stage pattern as Dockerfile.base-cpu but uses requirements-light.txt. Even smaller footprint.
Key differences from cpu: - requirements-light.txt instead of requirements-cpu.txt - LABEL tier="light" instead of tier="cpu" - May not need gcc/python3-dev if all light deps are pure Python or have pre-built wheels. Test: if `uv pip install` fails without gcc, add it. Otherwise omit for smaller builder stage.

task.title: Commit: new base images
task.description: Message: "feat(docker): add Dockerfile.base-cpu and Dockerfile.base-light (no CUDA)"

phase.name: Switch Flow Dockerfiles to Tier-Correct Bases
phase.description: Update 4 flow Dockerfiles to use their tier-correct base image. Add tier validation test to test_dockerfile_flows.py.

task.title: Test: flow Dockerfiles use tier-correct base images
task.description:
Add test_flow_dockerfiles_use_correct_tier() to test_dockerfile_flows.py.
Define the canonical tier mapping as a module-level dict: TIER_MAPPING = { "Dockerfile.train": "minivess-base:latest", "Dockerfile.hpo": "minivess-base:latest", "Dockerfile.post_training": "minivess-base:latest", "Dockerfile.analyze": "minivess-base:latest", "Dockerfile.deploy": "minivess-base:latest", "Dockerfile.data": "minivess-base:latest", "Dockerfile.annotation": "minivess-base:latest", "Dockerfile.monailabel": "minivess-base:latest", "Dockerfile.acquisition": "minivess-base:latest", "Dockerfile.biostatistics": "minivess-base-cpu:latest", "Dockerfile.dashboard": "minivess-base-light:latest", "Dockerfile.dashboard-api": "minivess-base-light:latest", "Dockerfile.pipeline": "minivess-base-light:latest", }
For each entry: assert the Dockerfile contains "FROM {expected_base}".
ALSO update test_all_flow_dockerfiles_inherit_from_base to accept all three base image names (not just minivess-base:latest).
task.expected-result: FAIL (4 Dockerfiles still use wrong base)

task.title: Switch biostatistics to minivess-base-cpu:latest
task.description: In Dockerfile.biostatistics: FROM minivess-base:latest → FROM minivess-base-cpu:latest Add: LABEL tier="cpu"

task.title: Switch dashboard, dashboard-api, pipeline to minivess-base-light:latest
task.description:
In each Dockerfile: FROM minivess-base:latest → FROM minivess-base-light:latest Add: LABEL tier="light"
Files: - deployment/docker/Dockerfile.dashboard - deployment/docker/Dockerfile.dashboard-api - deployment/docker/Dockerfile.pipeline

task.title: Commit: switch flow Dockerfiles to tier-correct bases
task.description: Message: "feat(docker): switch biostatistics/dashboard/pipeline to tier-correct bases"

phase.name: Infrastructure: Build Targets, Scanning, Documentation
phase.description: Update Makefile build/scan/requirements targets, deployment/CLAUDE.md docs. Final staging test verification.

task.title: Test: Makefile has tier-aware build, scan, and requirements targets
task.description: Write tests in tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_makefile_tiers.py: 1. Makefile contains target 'build-base-cpu' 2. Makefile contains target 'build-base-light' 3. Makefile contains target 'build-bases' (builds all 3) 4. Makefile 'scan' target references all 3 base images 5. Makefile contains target 'requirements-tiers' (regenerates requirements files)

task.title: Update Makefile with tier-aware targets
task.description:
Add targets:
build-base-gpu: DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build -t minivess-base:latest \ -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base .
build-base-cpu: DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build -t minivess-base-cpu:latest \ -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base-cpu .
build-base-light: DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build -t minivess-base-light:latest \ -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base-light .
build-bases: build-base-gpu build-base-cpu build-base-light
requirements-tiers: uv export --frozen --only-group cpu --no-emit-project \ --output-file deployment/docker/requirements-cpu.txt uv export --frozen --only-group light --no-emit-project \ --output-file deployment/docker/requirements-light.txt
Update 'scan' target to include base-cpu and base-light.

task.title: Update deployment/CLAUDE.md with tier documentation
task.description:
Replace the "Three-Layer Docker Hierarchy" section with an expanded "Three-Tier Base Image Hierarchy" section showing the parallel tiers.
Include: - Tier mapping table (flows → base image → Layer 1) - Build commands (make build-bases, make requirements-tiers) - "Adding a new flow" guide (determine tier → FROM → LABEL → test) - "Changing a flow's tier" migration guide - Note about requirements-{tier}.txt regeneration after uv lock

task.title: Run full staging test suite
task.description: Run `make test-staging` to verify all tests pass with the new tier structure and QA removal. All 4000+ tests must pass (minus deleted QA test files).

task.title: Final commit + PR
task.description: Stage all remaining changes. Create PR closing #537. Title: "feat(docker): flow-specific base image tiers (GPU/CPU/Light)" Body: tier mapping table, image size savings, predecessor PRs.

review.summary:
Three specialist agents reviewed the v1.0 plan in parallel. All critical findings incorporated into v2.0:
R1 (Docker/MLSecOps): Python version mismatch fixed (3.13 not 3.12). Image size targets corrected. Security hardening parity confirmed.
R2 (Python Packaging): Build strategy completely revised. extract_tier_deps.py REPLACED with [dependency-groups] + uv export.
R3 (Test Architecture): QA blast radius enumerated (16 files). _EXCLUDED set updated. test_docker_per_flow.py added.

Round 2 verified all critical v1.0 fixes present in v2.0. Remaining findings (all MEDIUM/LOW) incorporated into v2.1:
R3: 3 additional QA blast-radius files missed: - src/minivess/orchestration/constants.py (EXPERIMENT_QA, FLOW_NAME_QA) - tests/v2/unit/test_deployments_yaml.py ("qa-flow") - Makefile scan target (qa in flow image loop, line 65)
R1: analyze flow gpu="no" contradicts CUDA need → fixed to gpu="yes-cdi" R1: Dockerfile.base LABEL says "Python 3.12" → fix noted in T1.4 R1: stale "qa" comment in docker-compose.flows.yml line 115 → fix in T1.4 R2: PYTHONPATH=/app/src must be in runner stage → added to build strategy note R2: --no-emit-project redundant with --only-group → harmless, kept for clarity

Round 3 found 1 CRITICAL + 3 HIGH issues. All incorporated into v2.2:
R1: CONVERGED — no critical or high issues. One MEDIUM: analyze service lacks GPU devices in docker-compose.flows.yml (pre-existing, out of scope).
R2: CRITICAL — dashboard flow imports tracking.py which has top-level `from minivess.serving.model_logger import log_single_model` pulling in torch. Tier C assignment invalid without breaking this chain. FIX: New tasks T1.5-T1.6 make the import lazy (inside log_model() method). Only 1 call site. No functional impact on training flows.
R3: CRITICAL — 8 additional files with "qa" references missed: - tests/v2/unit/test_flow_trigger.py (flow lists + count assertion) - tests/v2/unit/test_deployments_config.py (required set) - tests/v2/integration/test_docker_compose_volumes.py (TestQaService class) - tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_names.py (FLOW_NAME_QA import) - tests/unit/orchestration/test_constants.py (EXPERIMENT_QA + FLOW_NAME_QA) - src/minivess/orchestration/flows/pipeline_flow.py (FLOW_NAME_QA — hard ImportError) - src/minivess/orchestration/agent_interface.py (DeterministicQATriage dead code) - src/minivess/orchestration/CLAUDE.md + deployment/CLAUDE.md (docs) All added to blast-radius, T1.3 checks, and T1.4 modification list. Also: test_docker_per_flow.py has 3 more qa refs (lines 70, 94, 166-170). Pre-existing bug: test checks "python:3.12" added to T1.4 fixes.

Round 4 CONVERGED — no CRITICAL or HIGH issues found.
MEDIUM: Task count was wrong (22 → 20). Fixed. LOW-MEDIUM: 3 documentation files with stale QA-as-standalone-flow refs: - CLAUDE.md (4 locations) - README.md (2 locations) - deployment/prefect/work-pools.yaml (2 comments) Added to blast-radius and T1.4 modification list.
All round-3 fixes verified: - T1.5-T1.6 import chain fix correctly scoped - All 30 blast-radius files now listed - Pre-existing bug fixes in T1.4 - Task numbering correct
PLAN IS READY FOR EXECUTION.
